name: Provencal Sauteed Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 can (28 oz) diced peeled tomatoes with juices
6 Fresh thyme sprigs OR 1 tbsp Dried thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the chicken with salt and pepper.
2. Heat oil in a heavy large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
3. Working in batches, add chicken and brown on all sides, about 5 minutes per batch. Transfer chicken to a plate.
4. Pour off all but 2 tablespoons of fat from the Dutch oven. Add salt pork; saute until golden, about 5 minutes.
5. Add onion and saute until almost tender and golden, about 6 minutes.
6. Add tomatoes with their juices, broth, wine, thyme, garlic, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
7. Return chicken to the Dutch oven. Cover and simmer until chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes.
8. Transfer chicken to a large bowl. Add olives to the Dutch oven and boil until the mixture reduces to 3 cups, about 25 minutes.
9. Mix in basil. Return chicken to the Dutch oven and stir until heated through, about 3 minutes.
10. Transfer chicken to a shallow serving bowl. Spoon sauce over and serve.
From the Provence region of France. Bon Appetit/May 94
